content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5660        322-5660          322-2001          322-6441          322-6444

April 16, 1981


Robert E. Hendrix
County Administrative Officer
County of Humboldt
Eureka, CA 95501

Dear Mr. Hendrix:

     This letter is in response to your request for information
as to our procedures for responding to inquiries and/or
complaints. You posed four specific questions:

     1.  What inquiries or complaints have been received
from Humboldt County and who originated the inquiries
or complaints?

     2.  What organizations or individuals are currently
under investigation in Humboldt County?

     3.  What are the procedures for responding to
inquiries and for conducting investigations?

     4.  What findings, if any, have been made to date?

     The Fair Political Practices Commission is the agency
charged with "primary responsibility for the impartial and
effective administration of the Political Reform Act." The
Commission is empowered to carry out its task by, among
other things, issuing advice and advisory opinions and imposing sanctions for violations of the Act. Our procedures
for responding to inquiries are very different from our
enforcement procedures. Government Code Section 83112
(copy attached) describes the general procedures followed
for issuance of advice and opinions. The Commission has
provided further guidance on this matter by regulation in 2
Cal. Adm. Code Sections 18320-18329. In the instant case,
we received a request for advice from your county counsel

_________________________________

1/  All statutory references made are to the Government
Code unless otherwise stated.

Robert E. Hendrix
April 16, 1981
Page Two


as to the application of the Act to Planning Commissioners
acting on the Local Coastal Plan. Within the last six months
we have received other advice requests from an individual
Planning Commissioner, a member of the Board of Supervisors,
a member of the Harbor Commission and from the Eureka city
attorney on behalf of the City Council.2/

Our procedures for responding to these requests for
advice have been no different from our normal procedures,
however, since the facts involved have been far more complex
and voluminous than those we typically encounter, we have
had to spend more time than usual researching the issues.
Part of our research included personal interviews with some
of the people knowledgeable about the issues. (Time con-
straints forced us to cancel several interviews, including
the one we had scheduled with you.)

I have already responded by letter to the advice requests
from the Planning Commissioner, the Supervisor and the Harbor
Commissioner. I expect responses to the county counsel and
the city attorney to be in the mail shortly.

Our enforcement procedures are described in Sections
91000-91014 of the Act (attached). Information as to potential
enforcement actions is confidential.

You say that many local elected officials have the
feeling that they may be under an "unnamed suspicion" and
"under investigation by unknown agencies." This is indeed
unfortunate, particularly since this agency has been straight-
forward in explaining to news media and other interested
parties the nature of our current involvement in Humboldt
County. I hope that this letter will dispel any unfounded
rumors and allay the fears you expressed in your letter.

Please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901 if you
have further questions in this regard.

Very truly yours,

Stella Connell Levy
Counsel
Legal Division

STC:kp
Attachments

2/ Since we receive hundreds of inquiries from throughout
the State, I cannot say whether or not there have been other
inquiries from your area.

sections.question:
1. What inquiries or complaints have been received from Humboldt County and who originated the inquiries or complaints?

2. What organizations or individuals are currently under investigation in Humboldt County?

3. What are the procedures for responding to inquiries and for conducting investigations?

4. What findings, if any, have been made to date?
sections.conclusion: The Fair Political Practices Commission is empowered to carry out its task by issuing advice and advisory opinions and imposing sanctions for violations of the Act. Our procedures for responding to inquiries are very different from our enforcement procedures. Government Code Section 83114 describes the general procedures followed for issuance of advice and opinions. The Commission has provided further guidance on this matter by regulation in 2 Cal. Adm. Code Sections 18320-18329. Information as to potential enforcement actions is confidential.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asks for information about complaints received from Humboldt County, who is under investigation, FPPC procedures for handling inquiries and investigations, and any findings made to date.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC explains that its advice procedures are governed by Government Code Section 83114 and administrative regulations, while enforcement procedures are confidential. The agency has been straightforward in its involvement in Humboldt County and has responded to multiple advice requests from local officials.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is FPPC response to administrative inquiry rather than a traditional advice letter on a legal question. Questions and responses are presented but not in traditional 'QUESTION' and 'CONCLUSION' sections. Statutory references and procedures detailed. Some ambiguity about what constitutes formal 'conclusion' as this is more of an informational response letter.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: 1. What inquiries or complaints have been received from Humboldt County and who originated the inquiries or complaints?

2. What organizations or individuals are currently under investigation in Humboldt County?

3. What are the procedures for responding to inquiries and for conducting investigations?

4. What findings, if any, have been made to date?

CONCLUSION: The Fair Political Practices Commission is empowered to carry out its task by issuing advice and advisory opinions and imposing sanctions for violations of the Act. Our procedures for responding to inquiries are very different from our enforcement procedures. Government Code Section 83114 describes the general procedures followed for issuance of advice and opinions. The Commission has provided further guidance on this matter by regulation in 2 Cal. Adm. Code Sections 18320-18329. Information as to potential enforcement actions is confidential.

embedding.summary: FPPC counsel responds to Humboldt County Administrative Officer's four questions about complaints, investigations, and procedures by explaining the distinction between advice procedures (which are public) and enforcement procedures (which are confidential), and providing statutory references.
